title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: **Idempotency risks** - `backup-record` defaults `completed_at` to `utc_now_iso()`, so retried/duplicated hook calls generate different `record_id`s and would emit duplicate Project OS artifacts for the same logical completion event ([cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py), [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - Scan replay (`stat.st_size < offset => offset=0`) can re-read old session events; DB dedupe may hold, but any Project OS write path not keyed to the same uniqueness rules will duplicate artifacts on replay ([task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - DB insert and journal append are coupled, but Project OS write is not currently in that transaction boundary; adding it naïvely creates split-brain states (DB says recorded, Project OS missing; or Project OS written, DB insert rejected) ([task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - Dispatch token changes per attempt; if Project OS artifact naming follows attempt tokens, retries become non-idempotent by construction ([task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - Writing `planning_diversity_evidence`/`implementation_subagents` from a single-lane backup event can encode false multi-agent evidence; that is a workflow-violation risk when fallback is single-lane ([PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md)). **Lock contention risks** - Lock contention is fail-open/skip (`exit 0` on busy lock in both scan and record paths), so Project OS artifacts can be silently dropped under concurrent hooks/timers ([run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh)). - Lock is `mkdir` + `trap` only; abnormal termination can leave stale lock dirs and cause prolonged silent skips ([run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh)). - SQLite writes in task backup do not implement explicit busy-retry policy; if lock discipline is bypassed (e.g., mixed `STATE_ROOT`/shared DB), `database is locked` failures can abort runs and lose Project OS writes ([task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - GitHub workflow concurrency is per `record_id`, not global branch; concurrent records pushing shared Project OS files to one branch can conflict and drop updates without deterministic merge/retry safety ([kernel-task-completion-backup.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/kernel-task-completion-backup.yml)). **Path safety risks** - `--cwd` is accepted/stored with normalization but no trusted-root enforcement; if reused as a write base for `.project-os`, arbitrary-path writes become possible ([cli.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/cli.py), [task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - Scan mode reads `cwd` from Codex state DB metadata without root-boundary validation; extending writes to that path risks writing outside intended repos ([task_backup.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tools/codex-kernel-guard/src/codex_kernel_guard/task_backup.py)). - Existing path sanitization is strong for backup mirror/receipt paths in GHA, but no equivalent guard exists yet for local `.project-os/...` targets, so traversal/symlink breakout risk is high if added directly ([kernel-task-completion-backup.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/kernel-task-completion-backup.yml), [PROJECT-OS.md](/Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/PROJECT-OS.md)). - Current tests cover backup dispatch/reconcile behavior but do not cover Project OS write idempotency, lock contention, or path-boundary enforceme...
